---
name: Task
about: Track a piece of planned work (feature, chore, refactor, etc.) to be picked up later
title: ''
labels: ['to refine']
type: Task
assignees: ''
---

<!--
Use this template to track planned work — a feature to build, a chore, a
refactor, or anything else that isn't a bug report. See the development
workflow for how work gets picked up:
https://openstef.github.io/openstef/contribute/development_workflow.html
-->

## Type of work

<!-- Check one. -->

- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Chore / refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Performance
- [ ] Epic (spans multiple issues)

## Summary

<!--
What needs to be done, and why? ("As a ..., I want ..., so that ..." is fine
if it helps, but a plain description works too.)
-->

## Background

<!--
Context that helps a contributor understand the "why": what prompted this,
links to related discussions/issues/docs, current behavior vs. desired
behavior.
-->

## Priority

<!--
What happens if this isn't done? Any deadlines or dependencies driving the
timing? Leave blank if there's no particular urgency.
-->

## Affected package(s)

<!-- Check all that apply. -->

- [ ] openstef-core
- [ ] openstef-models
- [ ] openstef-beam
- [ ] openstef-foundation-models
- [ ] openstef-meta
- [ ] examples / deployment
- [ ] docs
- [ ] not sure

## Definition of done

**Acceptance criteria** — what does "done" look like?

- [ ]
- [ ]

**Documentation** — does this need user guide, API reference, or example updates?

- [ ] No documentation changes needed
- [ ] Documentation will be updated as part of this work

**Tests** — how will this be verified?

- [ ] No new tests needed
- [ ] Unit/integration tests will be added

## Dependencies

<!-- Blocking issues, related PRs, or prerequisite work. Leave blank if none. -->

<!--
Maintainers will triage this with the appropriate labels (feature, chore,
enhancement, good first issue, ready-to-implement, ...) during refinement.
-->
